correction_notes: CORRECTED SCORE. Original V18 JSON (score_claude_V18_001) incorrectly listed video_filename as 'V_8.mov' and estimated 145s/451 FLS. ChatGPT native video analysis (37min thinking, 0.64 confidence) placed completion at 162.9s/433.6 FLS. Claude frame review confirms ChatGPT timing is more accurate. V_8.mov is a DIFFERENT video (149s, SURG20 wall-mount) now scored separately as V_8_video.
recording_notes: Phone filming desk-mounted RCA monitor at SURG25 station. Pegboard with instruments visible to left. 'SURG 25 - FLS Circle Cut Training and Testing' instruction sheet pinned above monitor. Pink/red foam block with Penrose drain. Dark (black) suture. 170s total video duration.

frame_analyses.description: First instrument enters field from left. TIMING STARTS ~3s absolute.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled entry. SURG25 desk RCA station - pegboard, instruction sheet.

frame_analyses.description: Needle being reoriented near drain. Several small regrasp maneuvers.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle loading deliberate rather than fluid - costs ~15s total.

frame_analyses.description: Needle tip engaged at target area, first pass underway through drain tissue.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry angle acceptable. Tissue handling gentle.

frame_analyses.description: Needle has traversed drain. Suture being drawn through second side.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pass appears close to target marks. Placement completing.

frame_analyses.description: First throw being configured with double-wrap appearance before cinching.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgeon's knot setup. Slowed by regrasping.

frame_analyses.description: First throw seated down onto drain. Approximation maintained.
Alternating loop formed for second throw. Suture wrapping visible.
Second throw being cinched. Both instruments applying tension across knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot control stable. 47.9s - major time sink.

frame_analyses.description: Final throw being formed after additional tail repositioning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw segment is slowest part (49.3s).

frame_analyses.description: Knot stack seated. Tails oriented for cutting.
Cutting instrument dividing remaining suture tails inside trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting completed intrabody. 6.7s - fastest cutting in the series.

knot_assessments.notes: Right-led double throw consistent with surgeon's knot.
Alternating single throw with left instrument leading. 47.9s - primary time bottleneck.
Final single throw alternates back to right. Secure. 49.3s.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Both ChatGPT and Claude frame review confirm no visible gap. Complete closure. Drain not avulsed.

technique_summary: V18 at 163 seconds is technically complete but inefficient. The main performance limiter is throw efficiency: second throw (47.9s) and third throw (49.3s) consume ~97s combined - 60% of total task time. These are dominated by repeated regrasping and prolonged tail management. The bite is acceptable, the knot stack is secure, and cutting is actually the FASTEST in the series at 6.7s. This suggests dramatic improvement in cutting mechanics but regression on throw efficiency at the unfamiliar SURG25 station.
improvement_suggestions: Throws 2+3 at 97s combined are the PRIMARY bottleneck - 60% of total time. Cutting is NOT the problem here.
Keep one tail under consistent tension; advance throws directly to knot - reduce regrasping pauses.
Needle loading at 14.9s is 3x slower than peak (5s) - station unfamiliarity.
The 6.7s cutting time proves fast cutting is achievable - transfer skill to throw mechanics.
CRITICAL DIAGNOSTIC: Run's bottleneck (throws) is completely different from most other runs (cutting).
strengths: FASTEST CUTTING in the entire series at 6.7s - cutting skill has been mastered
No visible gap - complete drain closure
Knot secure with all three throws in correct sequence
Drain not avulsed - safe tissue handling
Cross-validated by ChatGPT native video analysis (37min thinking, 0.64 confidence)

correction_log.reason: Original score used wrong video file. ChatGPT native analysis provided more accurate timing.
